reasoning: The historical WRBWFRBL data shows week-to-week volatility with a slightly downward overall trend due to ongoing balance sheet normalization (QT) and potential Treasury General Account (TGA) build-ups ahead of the tax season in April. Since quarter-end dynamics on March 31st (Tuesday) primarily affect exactly that day (with RRP spiking), the Wednesday April 1st level will likely see a reversal of any quarter-end drop, but relative to March 25, the structural trend and TGA build are more relevant. The FRED linear model gives a 34% chance of increase based on the short-term trend, though seasonality prior years hints at a possible start-of-month bump. Balancing these factors, I give a slightly below even probability (45%) of an increase.
